objective: Fix fork-local RunPod Docker build failures in the nightly vLLM install step while preserving explicit nightly opt-in behavior and keeping rollback simple.
scope: Apply minimal reversible edits in Dockerfile and aiwf.request.json only; keep stable install path unchanged, avoid upstream interaction, and preserve default nightly mode semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile nightly branch first attempts exact install of vllm==${VLLM_NIGHTLY_VERSION} from PyPI + wheels.vllm.ai/nightly.
If the pinned nightly install fails (e.g., wheel pruned or transient index error), Dockerfile retries pinned install once before falling back to latest nightly pre-release from the nightly index.
VLLM_NIGHTLY=true continues to enforce nightly-channel installs only; stable path remains gated behind VLLM_NIGHTLY=false.
No unrelated files or upstream remotes are modified.
rollback_plan: Fork-local rollback is a single commit that restores the previous nightly install line in Dockerfile (remove retry/fallback grouping) and reverts aiwf.request.json to its prior request record. No upstream interactions.
deploy_plan: Run py_compile, aiwf.request.json JSON parse check, and docker buildx bake --print in this fork; then build one nightly image path on RunPod and confirm vLLM import/startup before release tagging.
threat_model: Risks: fallback to latest nightly can introduce non-deterministic dependency resolution when pinned wheel is unavailable, and broad fallback on install failure can hide root-cause drift. Mitigations: keep fallback confined to VLLM_NIGHTLY=true path, retain exact pinned attempt first (with retry), preserve explicit stable rollback path via VLLM_NIGHTLY=false, and keep rollback to one Dockerfile hunk revert.
